title: Lingelsheimia gilgiana
taxonomy: Lingelsheimia gilgiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The exact classification of Lingelsheimia gilgiana may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified as a member of the Rubiaceae family. The classification of this plant can be further refined into its genus and species. Further research is necessary to determine the specific classification within the Rubiaceae family.

The plant is reported to have medicinal properties, with different preparations being used to treat various health conditions. In some regions, Lingelsheimia gilgiana is used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for its perceived health benefits. side_effects: Lingelsheimia gilgiana is a plant species that may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and vomiting when ingested due to its toxicity. Its sap can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. There is limited information available on the long-term effects of exposure to the plant.
